title: Quantitative Capstone — quantitative arm of the AI-corruption capstone study
public_claim: A quantitative future-work branch of the submitted qualitative capstone, not the submitted capstone itself.
distinctive_mechanism: It turns the Cognitive Firewalls question into controlled condition logic: a fifty-scenario, two-dose, three-protocol matrix with clean versus contaminated evidence, D0/D1 document pairs, DP1/DP2 packets, Scenario Factory materials, Blue/Red role architecture, DV-B contamination-handling logic, Baseline/ACH/CHAMPS protocol comparisons, scorer-skeleton logic, reliability checks, prompt and provenance records, and bounded interpretation rules.
use_value: It shows experimental-design capacity without pretending that unrun or reliability-limited quantitative machinery has already produced validated findings; floated runset options remain future-work options until adopted and executed.

minimum_public_demo: Test one representative case; confirm input (packet name, source files, version signal, scope...) produces output (what the packet is and what decision it can still...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.
claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
